hooks.command:
jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' | grep -qE '^git (commit|add)' && git diff --cached --name-only 2>/dev/null | grep -qE '(Migrations/|Entities/|DbContext)' && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"STOP: You are committing database changes. Before proceeding, verify ALL of the following:
1. Migration files are in src/Humans.Infrastructure/Migrations/ (NOT Data/Migrations/)
2. Migration namespace is Humans.Infrastructure.Migrations
3. Migration file has NOT been hand-edited — no migrationBuilder.Sql() calls, no manual column changes
4. Read the migration .cs file and verify EF did not generate incorrect RenameColumn (should be DropColumn + AddColumn for unrelated columns)
5. If EF generated something wrong, run dotnet ef migrations remove and regenerate — do NOT hand-edit
6. The model snapshot (HumansDbContextModelSnapshot.cs) is consistent
Do NOT proceed with the commit until you have verified all items above. Read the migration file NOW if you have not already."}}' || true

jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' | grep -qE 'dotnet ef migrations' && jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""' | grep -qE '(output-dir|Data/Migrations)' && echo 'BLOCKED: Do NOT use --output-dir with dotnet ef migrations. The default output directory (Migrations/) is correct. Using Data/Migrations/ has caused production issues. Remove the --output-dir flag and re-run.' && exit 1 || true
